goal: Pre-seeded broken articulation (zero-mass root, missing CollisionAPI on Link0, infinite joint limits). Diagnose with check_physics_health, fix each, verify clean.
thoughts: Diagnosis-then-fix loop: check_physics_health returns a structured issue list (prim path + severity + fix). For each issue, apply the suggested fix via apply_api_schema or set_attribute. Re-run check_physics_health to confirm. The reply must name issues specifically — generic 'fixed everything' is a red flag the agent didn't actually enumerate.
code:
# Step 1: diagnose
check_physics_health()
# Returns issues like:
#   {'prim': '/World/Arm', 'severity': 'critical', 'issue': 'Invalid mass: 0.0'}
#   {'prim': '/World/Arm/Link0', 'severity': 'error', 'issue': 'Mesh has RigidBodyAPI but no CollisionAPI'}
#   {'prim': '/World/Arm/Joint0', 'severity': 'warning', 'issue': 'Joint limits effectively infinite'}

# Step 2: fix each
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Arm", attr_name="physics:mass", value=1.0)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Arm/Link0", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Arm/Joint0", attr_name="physics:lowerLimit", value=-180.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Arm/Joint0", attr_name="physics:upperLimit", value=180.0)

# Step 3: verify clean
check_physics_health()
failure_modes: Running check_physics_health, seeing issues, but only fixing ONE of them and declaring 'fixed'.
Claiming 'fixed everything' without the re-check — verify-contract doesn't catch this directly but the judge's criteria-misses will.
Trying to fix via run_usd_script instead of the purpose-built tools — works but bypasses the measurable tool-chain contract.
Applying CollisionAPI to /World/Arm instead of /World/Arm/Link0 — wrong target.
